Module 2:
Tell the Story
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	2
Business Narrative
What story really is
Business narratives accomplish specific goals. First, what
they don’t do:
> not so people like you
> not to develop rapport
> not to help understanding
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	3
Narrative Transport
Navigating the ecosystem
Your target audience or buyer does not make decisions on
his own.
He talks with the people in his ecosystem. Narrative
transport insures he talks about the right things.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	4
Psychological Realism
Anchor business in reality
Communicating your business narrative in human time
scales and with concrete, primal human objectives makes
the business feels real.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	5
Paradigmatic Mode
Turn off cold analysis
A business narrative properly told activates a different side of
your target’s brain and puts them in the narrative mode.
The narrative mode encourages empathy, understanding, and
trust.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	6
Recapture Attention
Trade abstract for human
When attention wanders because of too many abstract notions,
narratives reengage your audience.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	7
Stories vs Arguments
Critical differences
A well formed argument convinces people that something is
likely to happen -- They establish emperical proof.
A good story convinces people that what you are talking about
is true -- They establish truthfulness.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	8
Future Action Projection
Brings audience into your vision
A business narrative is about what is going to happen.
Build on the past to create a vision of the future and credibly
bring your audience along .
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	9
Recap
Business narrative
The business narrative must:
> build on the past
> introduce characters people want to succeed
> show how those characters harness changes and provide
insight on those changes
> bring audience in on those changes and make them a part of
your vision of the future
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	10
Introduce the Big Idea
Establish the stakes
Business narratives have two characters: You and the business.
The big idea introduces you as the compelling character.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	11
Deepen the Problem
Make it real
Making problem come alive with a mini example of it.
And do not gloss over. Deepen it and dig into it.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	12
Intro the Character
Outline involvement
Describing your relationship and involvement with this
problem, a problem with large consequences, makes you a
compelling character.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	13
Tie to the Earth
Concrete, human scales
Buyers live in the real world with real stakes.
Two ways to anchor:
> admit other solutions to the problem
> tell a more traditional story that demonstrates impact on real
people’s lives.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	14
State Value Proposition
Idea introduction pattern
“For [target customers]
who are dissatisfied with [the current offerings in the market],
My Idea/product is a [new idea or product category]
that provides [key problems/solutions features].
Unlike [the competing product]
my idea/product is [describe the key features].”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	15
Be Warned
Easy to get lost
When discussing value proposition, it is easy to get lost in
numbers, and other abstract notions.
Remember to bring it back to the earth.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	16
Big Animal Pictures
Human time frames
Despite compelling economics, you must still communicate
how this affects real lives.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	17
Future Action Projection
From past to present to future
When you first describe your project, give it context by
describing it against moving forces.
Then build on those forces to create a vision of the future and
bring your audience along.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	18
Add Dramatic Tension
Not Hollywood tension
This kind of dramatic tension is counterpressure on the buyer.
Invite the buyer to qualify himself, and subtly withdraw.
After so much value, the buyer will instinctively reach out.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION 	19
Business Narrative
Redux
1. Build a character people care about
2. Deepen the problem
3. Describe your involvement
4. Anchor this in reality
5. Deliver the value proposition clearly
6. Then bring it back down to human scale
7. Build future action projection
8. Finish with dramatic tension
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 42 --

Module 2:
Tell the Story
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

PitchMastery.com

1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012

-- 21 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
1. Build a Character People Care About
Your audience needs to know that you are a character of depth, substance and integrity. That you have
faced adversity and overcome it, and that you can rise to any occasion by working hard and making
thoughtful decision.
“When I first got here it was not easy.”
• Consistency
• Sacrifice
• Going into the unknown
• Working hard in the face of uncertainty
• Holding against the tide
• When things got good, maintained values
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
Most people do not invest enough time with the problem. It’s important to investigate
the problem before jumping too quickly to your solution (and what you are selling.)
Features and Benefits	Solution
Problem
Most people gloss over
the problem with a
simple description of it.
They then move right away
into talking about their own
solutions.
Then it’s on to the features
and benefits. At this point
it is basically selling.
The Old (and Ineffective) Approach
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
This model gives the audience a point of entry into the problem. It doesn’t overwhelm.
Then, once you have their attention, you take them deeper into the problem and explore
it more fully.
Our Solution
Generic Solution
Infection is
bad
Infection is
a massive
problem
Infection is
a massive
problem AND
very hard to solve…
Infection is
really bad
Point of entry
Most people start here,
which overwhelms the
croc brain
BUT…
The New (and Effective) Approach
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
3. Describe Your Involvement
How are you connected to this problem?
“I’m deeply connected to this problem because my life has been about
elegant engineering solutions.”
“My father passed away. I’m committed to fixing this part of the
healthcare system.”
“For 10 years I made these presentation mistakes. I’m passionate about
showing others how to avoid them”
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
Tell a brief and easy to follow story of an individual that is affected by the problem.
See the Chelexa example...
Our wound care solution
can heal a soldier without
the risks common to battle
field injuries.
Also, discuss your activities in human time scales. Here are examples.
Doctors need a faster
method to contain and kill
germ growth within a
wound.
When Smith sustained a
gunshot to the arm, his
unit applied a wound
dressing within 3 minutes.
A doctor can identify a
wound, then: open the
package; break the seal,
unfold the dressing and
apply it ... with no training.
Don’t Say This 	Say This
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
This idea introduction pattern has been used successfully in thousands of deals. Geoffrey Moore
developed it in his seminal work, Crossing the Chasm. We couldn’t find a way to improve on it
(although we tried) and use it as originally written:
Here are examples from our Velocity pitch decks, ND1 and Chelexa.
“For [target customers]
who are dissatisfied with [the current offerings in the market],
My Idea/product is a [new idea or product category]
that provides [key problems/solutions features].
Unlike [the competing product]
my idea/product is [describe the key features].”
Idea Introduction Pattern
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 31 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
2. Deepen the Problem
1. Build a Character People Care About
3. Describe Your Involvement
4. Anchor the Problem in Reality
5. Deliver the Value Proposition Clearly
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
8. Add Dramatic Tension
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 32 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
Often the discussion of conceptual and abstract topics such as market size, earnings before tax
and depreciation, and other mathematical jargon will trigger the paradigmatic mode. This leads to
cold analysis. Keep your explanations ground in the physical:
Genetic Diagnostics:
How a sample moves through the lab
Patient provides
saliva sample
Kit is FedExed to
processing lab
Robotic arms
collect DNA
DNA is extracted
and analyzed
A diagnostic
report is
generated
The report is
emailed to
patient
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 33 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
6. Bring It Back to Human Timescales
This technique is especially useful when explaining complex models or theories.
Capital Management:
Catching the Black Swan Event
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
7. Construct the Future Action Projection
Build from the past, use trends in the present, and create the vision of the future.
Economic forces. Briefly describe what has changed financially in the market for
your big idea. Our customers wealthier, his credit more available, are there
increases or decreases in interest rates, etc.
Social forces. Highlight what emerging changes in people’s behavior patterns
exist for your big idea.
Technological forces. Technological change can flatten existing business models.
What changes have occurred on the technology side for your big idea.
Here is an example from a healthcare deck...
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
A Market Disruption
INCREASE OF
ADVERSE HEALTH
CONDITIONS IN THE
POPULATION
GENETIC DIAGNOSTIC MARKET SIZE
IDENTIFY
PATIENTS IN
ADVANCE OF
SYMPTOMS
AVOID
ADVERSE
REACTIONS
PATIENTS TAKE DRUGS
THAT ARE EFFECTIVE
DROP IN
COST OF
DIAGNOSTICS
$20B
INVESTMENT
IN GENETIC/
DISEASE RESEARCH
$4.4 B in 2010 $21 B in 2020
FOCUS
IDENTIFY
AVOID
KEY MARKET DRIVERS VALUE IS SHIFTING IN VERY LARGE MARKETS:
$100s OF BILLIONS AT STAKE
IN HEALTHCARE, DRUGS & DIAGNOSTICS
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

Sunday, May 6, 2012

-- 40 of 42 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
8. Add Dramatic Tension
There will be a dramatic tension when you tell the buyer/audience that even if
they want your service, product or idea, they can’t until you say so.
“We have the best product. Our customers love the value, the
competition doesn’t offer the same features. What we have is part of
the change that’s taking place. In the new world order, this is an
important piece of the future.
So were not here to explore validation of the product or service. We
want to focus on, ‘Is the timing right for us to work together? Is the
chemistry right?’”
More scripts are available to you in the Scripts section of this site.
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 41 of 42 --

Module 2:
Tell the Story
Sunday, May 6, 2012
www.getwsodownload.com
